caseStudy.title: Ads Fraud Detection System
caseStudy.description: Real-time invalid traffic detection protecting ad spend.
caseStudy.overview: An ad-tech platform was experiencing significant invalid traffic affecting campaign performance metrics and eroding advertiser trust. The rule-based detection system was reactive, catching fraud patterns only after significant spend had been wasted.
caseStudy.problem: Sophisticated bot networks mimicked human browsing behavior, evading rule-based filters. Advertisers were reporting discrepancies between platform metrics and third-party measurement tools, leading to disputes and churn.
caseStudy.solution: A multi-layered anomaly detection system was built, combining device fingerprinting, behavioral biometrics, and graph-based network analysis to identify coordinated invalid traffic patterns. Real-time scoring allowed suspicious traffic to be filtered before impressions were served, with human review queues for edge cases.

results.summary: Advertiser confidence in platform metrics was restored, reversing a trend of measurement-related churn. The system's continuous learning loop means it adapts to new fraud patterns as they emerge.
